scenario.title: Third ADH Claim Filed Near Environmental Exposure Threshold with Mixed Damage Indicators
scenario.description: Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term after two prior ADH claims were serviced under the AppleCare+ plan. The new incident involves intermittent screen flickering combined with subtle internal circuitry anomalies traced to a brief, unexpected condensation exposure during a moderate outdoor rain shower. Customer asserts this event was sudden and unintentional, occurring despite use of a water-resistant case. Apple’s diagnostics report both moisture and mechanical stress indicators without a definitive single causal factor, raising interpretation challenges on whether this constitutes accidental damage versus gradual environmental degradation.

service_fee.notes: Service fee does not apply since coverage is no longer provided after two ADH Service Events.

policy_references.snippet: ADH coverage will expire and all of Apple's obligations to you under this section 3.2 will be fulfilled before the end of the Plan Term when Apple, as a result of ADH claims, has provided to you two (2) Service Events.
You will pay a $49 (U.S.) dollar service fee plus applicable tax for each ADH claim.
If you cancel this Plan within thirty (30) days of your Plan receipt, you will receive a full refund less the value of any service provided under the Plan.

scenario.reasoning: The policy explicitly limits ADH coverage to two Service Events within the Plan Term (Section 3.2). Since the customer already exhausted these two ADH claims, this third damage incident falls outside the plan's coverage and thus does not require payment of the $49 service fee. Although the mechanical failure qualifies as an accidental damage event, the policy's numeric limit creates a coverage denial. However, the customer may still be eligible for hardware service claims under Section 3.1 if applicable. Customer will need to verify prior claim history and acknowledge no further ADH claims count or incur service fees.
scenario.adjustments: Specified that liquid exposure was brief and occurred outdoors during moderate rain, with water-resistant case in use; added intermittent screen flickering and subtle internal anomalies with mixed diagnostic indicators (moisture plus mechanical stress), lacking definitive causal confirmation.
scenario.risk_analysis: Shifting to an outdoor brief rain exposure mitigates the typical gradual environmental impact perception, while the water-resistant case use introduces doubt on damage cause. Mixed diagnostic results—moisture plus mechanical stress—cloud the determination if this qualifies as accidental damage or disallowed wear. These nuanced facts edge the scenario closer to the boundary of ADH coverage denial due to exceeding claim limits and cause ambiguities in applying the 'unexpected and unintentional external event' standard.

persona.defining quirks: Has a vast collection of antique books
persona.detailed job description: Retired university professor in the field of Fine Arts
persona.education: Professional degree beyond a bachelor's degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.lifestyle: Quiet and solitary, with a focus on intellectual and creative pursuits
persona.mannerisms: Often lost in thought, tends to speak in a poetic manner
persona.personal time: Spends free time reading, painting or visiting art galleries

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Third ADH Claim Filed Near Environmental Exposure Threshold with Mixed Damage Indicators

Scenario description:
Customer files a third accidental damage from handling (ADH) claim within the Plan Term after two prior ADH claims were serviced under the AppleCare+ plan. The new incident involves intermittent screen flickering combined with subtle internal circuitry anomalies traced to a brief, unexpected condensation exposure during a moderate outdoor rain shower. Customer asserts this event was sudden and unintentional, occurring despite use of a water-resistant case. Apple’s diagnostics report both moisture and mechanical stress indicators without a definitive single causal factor, raising interpretation challenges on whether this constitutes accidental damage versus gradual environmental degradation.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello there. I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It’s the 14 Pro Max, and I think it’s had some issues after a little rain I accidentally got caught in. It’s flickering and acting strange.
messages.raw_text: Hello there. I need to file a claim for my iPhone. It’s the 14 Pro Max, and I think it’s had some issues after a little rain I accidentally got caught in. It’s flickering and acting strange.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you. To start your claim, could you please provide the Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?
messages.raw_text: Thank you. To start your claim, could you please provide the Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone 14 Pro Max?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, I have the Plan Agreement Number right here. It's APLCARE-8754210392. Will that do?
messages.raw_text: Sure, I have the Plan Agreement Number right here. It's APLCARE-8754210392. Will that do?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Yes, thank you. Please describe the specific issue you're experiencing with your iPhone.
messages.raw_text: Yes, thank you. Please describe the specific issue you're experiencing with your iPhone.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: The screen flickers on and off, and there's some weird internal circuitry stuff happening. Like, it’s not fully broken but definitely acting up. I think the rain I was outside in briefly might have caused it, even though I use a water-resistant case. It’s just odd because it’s not totally dead, you know?
messages.raw_text: The screen flickers on and off, and there's some weird internal circuitry stuff happening. Like, it’s not fully broken but definitely acting up. I think the rain I was outside in briefly might have caused it, even though I use a water-resistant case. It’s just odd because it’s not totally dead, you know?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for describing the issue. Could you please tell me the model of your iPhone, the iOS version it is currently running, and if you have any peripherals attached? Also, if you saw any error messages or have already tried any troubleshooting steps, please share that information.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for describing the issue. Could you please tell me the model of your iPhone, the iOS version it is currently running, and if you have any peripherals attached? Also, if you saw any error messages or have already tried any troubleshooting steps, please share that information.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: My iPhone is the 14 Pro Max, with 512 GB storage. I don’t remember the iOS version exactly, but it's pretty up to date. I wasn’t using any peripherals when this started. No error messages, just the flickering and strange internal behavior. I’ve tried turning it off and on, but doesn’t really help.
messages.raw_text: My iPhone is the 14 Pro Max, with 512 GB storage. I don’t remember the iOS version exactly, but it's pretty up to date. I wasn’t using any peripherals when this started. No error messages, just the flickering and strange internal behavior. I’ve tried turning it off and on, but doesn’t really help.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing those details. Since the issue involves potential water exposure and abnormal behavior, I also need to ask if you can provide more information about how and where the incident occurred. Did the device drop or get liquid damage, and if so, could you describe the event in detail?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing those details. Since the issue involves potential water exposure and abnormal behavior, I also need to ask if you can provide more information about how and where the incident occurred. Did the device drop or get liquid damage, and if so, could you describe the event in detail?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yeah, it was a quick rain shower outside. I was walking across my yard, no drops or anything, but the rain was moderate and I had it in one of those water-resistant cases. Still, I guess some moisture got in or maybe some internal stuff was affected. It all happened so fast I didn't think much of it at first. Now it’s acting up, you see.
messages.raw_text: Yeah, it was a quick rain shower outside. I was walking across my yard, no drops or anything, but the rain was moderate and I had it in one of those water-resistant cases. Still, I guess some moisture got in or maybe some internal stuff was affected. It all happened so fast I didn't think much of it at first. Now it’s acting up, you see.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that. To proceed with your claim, I will need to gather some additional information. Would you like to provide supporting photos or logs of the incident, or is there anything else you would like to add before we move forward?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that. To proceed with your claim, I will need to gather some additional information. Would you like to provide supporting photos or logs of the incident, or is there anything else you would like to add before we move forward?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, I don't have any photos or logs. I just want to get this sorted out. It's frustrating to have my device acting up after such a minor thing. Please, let’s move forward with the claim.
messages.raw_text: No, I don't have any photos or logs. I just want to get this sorted out. It's frustrating to have my device acting up after such a minor thing. Please, let’s move forward with the claim.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Understood. Your claim will now be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we finish?
messages.raw_text: Understood. Your claim will now be processed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we finish?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that’s all for now. Thanks for helping me get this started. I just want to get it resolved quickly.
messages.raw_text: No, that’s all for now. Thanks for helping me get this started. I just want to get it resolved quickly.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it is complete. Thank you for contacting AppleCare. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified as soon as it is complete. Thank you for contacting AppleCare. Have a great day! <END>
